blocks.content: Complex software engineering problems cannot be solved efficiently by a single model loop. Splitting tasks across focused, specialized agents increases precision, reduces input context usage, and enables parallel processing pipelines.
Developers can configure agent communications using two primary structure topologies:

items.title: Hub-and-Spoke Topology
items.content: A central coordinator agent receives user input, plans steps, delegating tasks to sub-agents, compiling output.

items.title: Peer-to-Peer Mesh Topology
items.content: Agents communicate directly, triggering callbacks and passing payloads autonomously based on state changes.

blocks.content: The Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol defines how agents declare parameters, register endpoints, establish trust, and exchange structured JSON task payloads across local networks or public HTTP transports.
Question 1: Which multi-agent topology uses a coordinator agent to process user inputs and delegate tasks?

- A) Peer-to-Peer Mesh
- B) Hub-and-Spoke
- C) Circular Chain
Feedback: Option B is correct. In a Hub-and-Spoke model, a central coordinator coordinates routing and delegation between spokes.
